title: Source of Evas ATF Native Soul or Pilot
display_title: Source of Evas ATF Native Soul or Pilot
wikitext:
[]: Source of Eva's ATF (Native Soul or Pilot?)             Quote Post by Trajan » Thu Mar 24, 2011 3:05 pm [Split from here .] I've always had an interest regarding this. The pilots only seem to displace offensive AT Fields when the soul inside the Evangelion 'wakes up.' They can use it defensively or to neutralize Angel AT Fields, but they can only seem to use them to attack in this state. Any thoughts?

[]:             Quote Post by AshPhoenix » Thu Mar 24, 2011 3:10 pm Trajan wrote: I've always had an interest regarding this. The pilots only seem to displace offensive AT Fields when the soul inside the Evangelion 'wakes up.' They can use it defensively or to neutralize Angel AT Fields, but they can only seem to use them to attack in this state. Any thoughts?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Mar 26, 2011 5:16 am Trajan wrote: I've always had an interest regarding this. The pilots only seem to displace offensive AT Fields when the soul inside the Evangelion 'wakes up.' They can use it defensively or to neutralize Angel AT Fields, but they can only seem to use them to attack in this state. Any thoughts?

[]:             Quote Post by AshPhoenix » Sat Mar 26, 2011 11:35 am Reichu wrote: Now I'm gonna have to go through the cut frame-by-frame again...

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Mar 26, 2011 11:58 am AshPhoenix wrote: I think that the AT Field is originally produced by the pilot, and amplified by the Eva. Not to say that the Eva doesn't have an AT Field as well, just that (as I said earlier) the Eva probably doesn't exhibit its own AT Field until a certain "threshold of berserkness" is passed.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Sat Mar 26, 2011 12:09 pm I've always thought it was suggested that the pilot's AT field at least partially merges with that of the Eva while synced. Why else would damage done to the Eva affect the pilot?

[]:             Quote Post by AshPhoenix » Sat Mar 26, 2011 12:25 pm Reichu wrote: Out of curiosity, how are you reaching this conclusion? How do you reconcile Asuka's words about "the meaning of the A.T. Field" with it? What about dummy-plugged Evas generating A.T. Fields? The requirement that Evas require souls in the first place?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Mar 26, 2011 3:39 pm Azathoth wrote: I've always thought it was suggested that the pilot's AT field at least partially merges with that of the Eva while synced. Why else would damage done to the Eva affect the pilot?

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Sat Mar 26, 2011 3:44 pm Reichu wrote: Though, I wonder where Rei's line from episode 23 fits into this. Why would the ATF "disappear" if she ejects from Eva-00? There is a similar implication -- ATF vanishes with pilot -- at the end of 26', where Eva-01's ATF fades out at the same time Shinji's implied to have ejected.

[]:             Quote Post by AshPhoenix » Sat Mar 26, 2011 4:00 pm Reichu wrote: Was asking you to explain how you arrived at your conclusions I think that the AT Field is originally produced by the pilot, and amplified by the Eva and the Eva probably doesn't exhibit its own AT Field until a certain "threshold of berserkness" is passed -- what else?

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Sat Mar 26, 2011 4:25 pm AshPhoenix wrote: The Eva would take such a metaphorical AT Field, and amplify it to create a physical AT Field.

[]:             Quote Post by AshPhoenix » Sat Mar 26, 2011 5:27 pm Azathoth wrote: Human AT fields are hardly a metaphor within the world of Eva. They are quite literally what enables humans to keep their shape.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Mar 27, 2011 5:09 pm I still have misgivings over the "pilot's ATF augmented by Eva's" interpretation. I'll admit that there is some evidence that seems to be antithetical to a resolution, but, before anything else, let's revisit a very important point: AshPhoenix wrote: Asuka says that the AT Field is "mama protecting her" and whatnot, right? So that supports my theory: by connecting with Asuka, Kyoko is able to augment ("protect") Asuka's AT Field with her own.

[]:             Quote Post by AshPhoenix » Sun Mar 27, 2011 8:52 pm Reichu wrote: And this makes sense, considering the thematic analogy between the pilot and an embryo in the womb. Saying that the Eva's ATF sources primarily from the pilot doesn't seem to hold up in consideration of Asuka's words and the thematic implications in general.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Mar 27, 2011 9:09 pm AshPhoenix wrote: I'm saying that the AT Field that is most often projected (unless the Eva is berserk or in heightened sync mode or something) comes from the pilot's soul.

[]: !             Quote Post by Sik » Sun Mar 27, 2011 11:22 pm Maybe I can help, we know there are 2 types of ATF, Adan type and Lilin (human) type, Evangelions can use Adan type (can manifest in physical ways) since they're clones, but like Kaworu said (i think he was) "The AT field is the wall of your soul" that means u need a soul to use it, since Evangelions are like "bodies" and need a soul, they use pilots as souls, but they need to combine (synchronize) in order to use the Eva's fields, the stronger the synchronization is, the stronger the ATF will be. When a mother soul (Kyoko-Yui) "awakes" the sync reaches the highest potential and thats why Evas kick ass in those momments. So we could say pilots ATF are useless on battle right? When a pilot ejects from the plug hes interrupting the sync, that means, the Eva's capability to "use" his ATF. Am I right on this?

[]:             Quote Post by AshPhoenix » Mon Mar 28, 2011 3:42 pm Reichu wrote: I have a feeling that browsing the show for dialogue and computer screens would abundantly confirm that the ATF is attributed to the Evas (and not only during berserk incidents). Found one already.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon Mar 28, 2011 5:03 pm I'd say the people in the show understand the A.T. Field pretty well, well enough to not only detect them, but discern between who one belongs to. A few others: Episode 12 wrote: Misato: They'll be fine. Even if the Evas are badly damaged, their AT Fields will protect the kids. Inside an Eva is the safest place to be.

[]:             Quote Post by Sik » Mon Mar 28, 2011 6:07 pm just adding in the fact that the ATF sync means that the Eva is amplifying the pilot's ATF (i.e, "converting" it into an "Adam-type" ATF). And that going berserk creates a higher level of sync, per se, so that the mother soul can add in its own ATF to the mix and make a super-powerful ATF.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Mar 28, 2011 6:46 pm AshPhoenix wrote: Eva-00/Eva-01 wasn't in berserker mode at the time
